title: Model Manager list flashes on every re-sync (full renderList teardown)
description:
The model-downloads side panel (MpiModelManager) visibly FLASHES on almost any operation — opening the panel, after a download starts/completes/cancels/uninstalls, toggling an op, and every awaitReSync(). User reports it 'keeps flashing' and 'almost seems buggy'; the flash IS the refresh becoming visible.

ROOT CAUSE: renderList() in js/components/Compounds/LandingPages/MpiModelManager/MpiModelManager.js (~line 487) does a FULL teardown + rebuild every call — _destroyAllCards(), removes ALL .card/.section-header/.empty DOM, then re-creates every card from scratch via _buildCard(). It is triggered on: Events.on('state:changed') when key==='s_installedModelIds' (fires on EVERY models:checked after any re-sync, even when the installed SET did not change), download:started, download:complete, download:cancelled, download:uninstalled, download:failed, every op-toggle commit(), and onOpen.

Note: live PROGRESS ticks are already fine — download:progress patches the card in place via card.display.el.setProgress() (line ~524), no rebuild. The flash is purely the whole-list rebuild on re-sync/lifecycle events.

FIX (keyed/diff render): mirror the MpiQueuePanel signature-diff pattern (see memory [[project_queue_panel_render_diff]]). Reuse existing card instances, patch state in place (installed/partial/draft/button), add/remove only the cards that actually changed sections. Cheap interim win available if a full rewrite is too big: add a signature guard on the s_installedModelIds 'state:changed' handler so it only rebuilds when the installed set genuinely changed — that alone kills most incidental flashing.

CARE: card teardown lifecycle — _destroyAllCards() must still run for any card actually removed (cleanup contract). Op-toggle draft state (_setDraft / _opToggles / _cardInstances maps) must survive an in-place patch. Don't regress the partial-progress bar or the Install/Update/Uninstall button state machine (MPI-122).

Related: surfaced while fixing MPI-123 (remote cancel stale partial bytes) — the MPI-123 fix correctly adds a re-sync on cancel, which also flashes the list, same root cause.
